The Big Short (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $70,259,870Details
International Box Office $62,364,811
Worldwide Box Office $132,624,681
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $3,571,763
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,725,103
Total $7,296,866
Further financial details...

Synopsis

When four outsiders saw what the big banks, media and government refused to, the global collapse of the economy, they had an idea: The Big Short.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$705,527 (1.0% of total gross)
Legs:6.67 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:53.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$28,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:8 opening theaters/2,529 max. theaters, 5.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $98,011,267

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 11th, 2015 (Limited) by Paramount Pictures
December 23rd, 2015 (Expands Wide) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: December 23rd, 2015 (Wide) (France)
December 30th, 2015 (Wide) (Belgium)
December 30th, 2015 (Wide) (Switzerland)
December 31st, 2015 (Wide) (Lebanon)
December 31st, 2015 (Wide) (Oman)
Video Release: March 15th, 2016 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: R for pervasive language and some sexuality/nudity.
(Rating bulletin 2400 (Cert #50062), 11/18/2015)
Running Time: 130 minutes
Award nomination: 2016 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: Producers Guild of America Best Theatrical Motion Picture Winner, Writers Guild of America Best Adapted Screenplay Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Breaking the Fourth Wall, Epilogue, Gratuitous Cameos, Intertitle, Prologue, Voiceover/Narration
Geographical setting: Las Vegas
Plot point: Corporate Malfeasance, Death of a Sibling, Depression, Market Bubble, Suicide
Subgenre: Comedy Drama
Time period setting: 2000s
Source:Based on Factual Book/Article
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Plan B Entertainment, Regency Enterprises
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

When four outsiders saw what the big banks, media and government refused to, the global collapse of the economy, they had an idea: The Big Short.
